overview: Azorius Stoneblade is a Legacy control deck that leverages White and Blue's superior card selection and disruption to outlast opponents. It uses Stoneforge Mystic to fetch a stabilizing sword, Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student for massive card advantage, and Stifle to shut down key opponent spells. The goal is to establish a resilient board state and win through incremental value and targeted interaction.
win_conditions: Stoneforge Mystic fetching a sword to stabilize and generate value
Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student drawing into answers and outlasting the opponent
Stifle countering game-winning combo pieces or tutors to stall for time

keep_criteria: Hands containing Stifle or other early interaction
Two or more lands with a path to Stoneforge Mystic or Tamiyo
Hands that can respond to common Legacy opening plays
mulligan_criteria: Hands with no interaction or removal
Hands with three or more lands
Hands lacking a clear path to the sword or card advantage engine

early_game: Prioritize playing lands and holding up mana for Stifle
Use Stifle to counter fetch lands, tutors, or early combo pieces
Play Stoneforge Mystic as early as possible to set up the sword
mid_game: Fetch the designated sword using Stoneforge Mystic
Cast Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student to draw cards and find answers
Maintain a hand of interaction while developing board presence
late_game: Stabilize with the sword's defensive or value-generating abilities
Use Tamiyo's draw to out-resource the opponent
Close the game once the opponent runs out of threats or interaction

key_cards.name: Stoneforge Mystic
key_cards.notes: Play on curve and equip immediately to stabilize against early aggression or set up late-game value.

key_cards.name: Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student
key_cards.notes: Use its draw ability to refill your hand after interaction, and its tutor ability to find specific answers or the sword if needed.

key_cards.name: Stifle
key_cards.notes: Hold up mana to counter spells with mana costs, particularly fetch lands, tutors, or combo finishers, to disrupt the opponent's game plan.

sequencing_tips: Always hold up Stifle until you know what the opponent is casting
Play Stoneforge Mystic before casting Tamiyo to ensure the sword is available for defense
Use Tamiyo's draw ability when your hand is low and you need specific answers
Manage mana carefully to keep interaction available while developing board presence

matchups.advice: Use Stifle aggressively on tutors, fetch lands, or key combo pieces to buy time for Stoneforge and Tamiyo.
Prioritize early Stifle on threats if possible, but focus on playing Stoneforge quickly to fetch a defensive sword.
Race to establish the sword and use Tamiyo to draw out their interaction while holding Stifle for their win conditions.
Disrupt their early game with Stifle, then stabilize with the sword and outdraw them with Tamiyo.
Stifle their mana acceleration or tutors, then race to the sword before they assemble a large threat.
